title: COUNT(*) in a LEFT JOIN reports 1 for rows that matched nothing
rule: In a LEFT JOIN, unmatched left rows are padded with an all-NULL right row, and COUNT(*) still counts that phantom — so a parent with zero children reports 1, not 0. Use COUNT(<right_table_nonnull_column>) (e.g. COUNT(child.id)), which ignores the NULL filler and returns 0. Beware the same inflation when COUNT(*) feeds an AVG/ratio denominator.
triggers: LEFT JOIN count returns 1 instead of 0
customers with no orders show count 1
group with zero matches counted as one
